seoTitle: How to Create a Jira API Token in Minutes
metaDescription: Step-by-step guide to creating a new Jira API token from your Atlassian account for secure script and integration authentication.
intro: If you're building an integration or automation that needs to talk to Jira, you'll need an API token rather than logging in with a password. This guide covers creating a new token from your Atlassian account settings and storing it safely.

steps.heading: Go to account settings
steps.body: Click your profile avatar in the top right corner of Jira and select Account Settings.

steps.heading: Find the API token section
steps.body: Open the Security tab and scroll to the API token section, then click through to the credentials dashboard.

steps.heading: Create your token
steps.body: Click Create API Token, enter a descriptive label so you remember what it's for later, and click Create.

steps.heading: Copy the token immediately
steps.body: Click the copy button to grab the raw string and save it somewhere secure, since it disappears once you close the popup.

steps.heading: Manage or revoke tokens later
steps.body: Return to the credentials dashboard anytime to review your active tokens or revoke ones that are no longer needed.

faq.question: How do I create a Jira API token?
faq.answer: Open your Atlassian account settings, go to Security, click Create and Manage API Tokens, then click Create API Token and label it.

faq.question: What should I name my Jira API token?
faq.answer: Use a descriptive label that tells you what the token is being used for, since you may end up managing several tokens over time.

faq.question: Can I recover a lost Jira API token?
faq.answer: No, once you close the creation popup the raw token isn't shown again — you'll need to revoke it and create a new one.

faq.question: Is a Jira API token the same as my account password?
faq.answer: No, a token is a separate credential specifically for authenticating scripts and integrations without exposing your actual password.
